title: sarcococca hookeriana var. humilis
intro: Sarcococca hookeriana var. humilis, also known as Christmas box or sweet box, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Himalayas. The plant contains medicinal properties, including alkaloids and glycosides, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
